author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Manichæan Controversy.
Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
Fire is Called Eternal, Not as God Is, But Because Without End.

ccel.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

title: Fire is Called Eternal, Not as God Is, But Because Without End.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: But fire is eternal, not as God is eternal, because, though without end, yet is not without beginning; but God is also without beginning.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Then, although it may be employed perpetually for the punishment of sinners, yet it is mutable nature.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: But that is true eternity which is true immortality, that is that highest immutability, which cannot be changed at all.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: For it is one thing not to suffer change, when change is possible, and another thing to be absolutely incapable of change.

verses.book: Concerning the Nature of Good, Against the Manichæans.
verses.text: Therefore, just as man is called good, yet not as God, of whom it was said, "There is none good save God alone;" and just as the soul is called immortal, yet not as God, of whom it was said, "Who alone hath immortality;" and just as a man is called wise, yet not as God, of whom it was said, "To God the only wise;" so fire is called eternal, yet not as God, whose alone is immortality itself and true eternity.
